title: Data Retrieval
text: Data Retrieval Every DBMS connector recognizes the same set of connector I/O requests. When retrieving data from the database, Uniface selects the required data with the Select connector request, and fetches it into the component with the Fetch connector request. Uniface uses the Select request to create a partially built hitlist called a stepped hitlist. The most commonly used connector I/O requests include: S Select —select a group of occurrences that match the retrieve profile: it can include wildcards. The connector returns the hitlist, possibly in steps, and can return data if the select cache can be used. F Fetch —return the data for a single occurrence. If necessary, the connector interface includes a mode indicating that the occurrence should be locked. U Update —update the data for an existing occurrence in the DBMS. W Write —insert a new occurrence into the DBMS. D Delete —delete an existing occurrence from the DBMS. When retrieving data from a DBMS, Uniface uses the Select and Fetch connector I/O requests to locate the desired database occurrences and to bring the data into the component. When a request is made for data, either by the user or by a ProcScript instruction, the connector interface issues the Select request. The DBMS connector responds to this request by locating the requested occurrences in the database, using the retrieve profile as the argument. If no matching occurrence is found, the retrieve process stops and no more calls are made to the DBMS connector in the current attempt to retrieve data. If only one occurrence is found, Uniface sends a Fetch request to the DBMS connector to bring that occurrence into the form. Related Links Database Connector Requests Database connector requests are recognized by every Uniface DBMS connector. Each request is identified by an alphabetic character, such as O for Open or S for Select. You need to know these requests and the modes that some of them use, when analyzing I/O messages in the message frame. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
